name: Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges
name_original: Ponts - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Ponts
description: This moderate 21.2-mile touring cycling route near Ponts, Spain, explores historical towns and scenic landscapes with 1144 feet of elevation
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ponts, located in Catalonia, Spain, offer diverse terrain at the base of the Pre-Pyrenees. The region features quiet roads, varied gradients, and winding routes through the Segre River Valley. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging climbs and peaceful rural landscapes across central Catalonia. Best touring cycling routes around Ponts The most popular touring cycling route is Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges, a 21.2 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several villages through the rural landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Abandoned Village of Claret – Vilamajor Village loop from Ponts, a difficult 35.4 miles (57.0 km) path. This route explores historical villages and offers varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Les Pallargues Castle – Abandoned Village of Claret loop from Ponts, a 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail leading through historical sites and rural areas, often completed in about 6 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Ponts is defined by quiet roads, varied gradients of the Pre-Pyrenees, and routes along the Segre River Valley. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Ponts are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from 9 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ponts's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of touring cycling routes can I expect around Ponts?
A: Touring cycling around Ponts, located in Catalonia, Spain, offers a diverse landscape at the base of the Pre-Pyrenees. You'll find quiet roads, varied gradients, and routes winding through the Segre River Valley. The terrain ranges from challenging climbs in the Pre-Pyrenees to peaceful rural paths, providing a mix of experiences across central Catalonia.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in the Ponts area?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available around Ponts, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has highly rated these routes, with an average score of 4.25 stars from 9 reviews, and more than 200 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes in Ponts offer a moderate to difficult challenge, there are options that provide a more accessible experience. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968717317/bridges-oliola-cabanabona-vilanova-de-la-aguda-bridges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges&lt;/a&gt; is rated moderate and connects several villages through a rural landscape, making it a good choice for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes around Ponts?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Ponts offers several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Claret – Vilamajor Village loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.4 miles (57.0 km) and explores historical villages with varied terrain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31923210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Pallargues Castle – Abandoned Village of Claret loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt;, a 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail through historical sites and rural areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ponts?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Ponts are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968717317/bridges-oliola-cabanabona-vilanova-de-la-aguda-bridges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges&lt;/a&gt; loop and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Backside fun gravel road – Church of Vilanova de l'Aguda loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Ponts offer a blend of natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can cycle through the winding roads of the Pre-Pyrenees, along the Segre River Valley, and past significant historical sites. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5122254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Claret&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6222339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt;, and various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsonís Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/947499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montclar Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Segre River?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in Ponts wind through the Segre River Valley, offering a different landscape compared to the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Backside fun gravel road – Segre River loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic ride along this important waterway.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ponts?
A: Ponts, being at the base of the Pre-Pyrenees in Catalonia, Spain, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, making spring and autumn ideal for exploring the diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any specific routes that explore historical villages or castles?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history, and several routes are designed to take you through historical villages and past ancient castles. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Claret – Vilamajor Village loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31923210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Pallargues Castle – Abandoned Village of Claret loop from Ponts&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples, allowing you to combine cycling with cultural exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Ponts is varied, reflecting its location at the base of the Pre-Pyrenees. You'll encounter quiet asphalt roads, gravel sections, and paths that wind through valleys and along rivers. Expect a mix of ascents and descents, with some routes featuring significant elevation changes, providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring in Ponts?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ponts are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.25 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, the varied gradients of the Pre-Pyrenees, and the scenic routes along the Segre River Valley, highlighting the region's diverse and engaging cycling opportunities.
tips: Sant Joan Baptista is the parish church of the town located a little away from the center of the municipality, next to the cemetery. It was built with the help of the neighbors and currently, they are still in charge of taking care of it and paying the expenses.

Pretty town in the region of La Noguera, in the province of Lleida. In the towns that make up the municipality of Vilanova de la Aguda, we find castles, churches, hermitages and chapels with a great history.
Nice square in Ponts, Vila and municipality of the Noguera region, in Lleida. We will find very good restaurants such as Apagallums and the town hall.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 34.2 kilometers long and is estimated to take around 2 hours and 29 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is largely characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, as it's classified as 'moderate', some segments might be unpaved and could present a challenge, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass through scenic areas and towns with historical significance. Notable highlights include the Sant Joan Baptista Church of Cabanabona and the Church of Vilanova de l'Aguda. Vilanova de la Aguda itself features castles, churches, hermitages, and chapels with rich history. Oliola includes an abandoned town with a church dedicated to the Madre de Dios del Carme. The route is also in proximity to Les Pallargues Castle, part of the Sió Castles Route.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate', the route's 34.2 kilometers and 350 meters of elevation gain suggest it's best suited for touring cyclists with good fitness levels. Beginners might find some unpaved segments challenging.

faqs.question: Is the Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Komoot does not provide specific information regarding dog-friendliness for this particular touring bicycle route. It's always recommended to check local regulations and signage regarding pets in the areas you plan to cycle through, especially in towns and historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the province of Lleida, Spain. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler, so consider the weather forecast for your chosen time of visit.

faqs.question: Are there any specific parking areas or trailheads for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is named 'Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges', suggesting a start and end point near 'Bridges' (likely Ponts). While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for parking in or around Ponts to begin this circular route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: There are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public touring bicycle route. However, any specific attractions or historical sites you choose to visit along the way might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Etapa 45, Etapa 44, Camí de la Rovira a Maravella, and Camí de Ponts a Vilanova de l'Aguda, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns, including Oliola, Cabanabona, and Vilanova de la Aguda, as well as starting and ending near Ponts. These settlements are likely to offer opportunities to find cafes, pubs, or other establishments for food and drink.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route name 'Bridges - Oliola - Cabanabona - Vilanova de la Aguda - Bridges' indicates that it is a circular route, starting and ending in the same general area.
